title: Optoelectronics and Magnetic properties calculation of RE2MnNiO6 (RE=La-Lu,Y) using Density Functional Theory
abstract: RE2NiMnO6 (RE = La-Lu) family of ordered double-perovskite oxides hosts a corner-sharing network of alternating NiO6 and MnO6 octahedra whose electronic and magnetic ground states are systematically governed by the A-site ionic radius through the lanthanide contraction. The strong localisation of RE 4f electrons poses a fundamental challenge to density-functional treatments, yet the hybridisation between 4f and neighbouring 5d (RE) or 3d (Ni, Mn) states is central to the origin of exchange interactions and optoelectronic response across the series. We present a comprehensive first-principles study of the electronic structure, lattice dynamics, and optical properties of representative RE2NiMnO6 compounds within the DFT+U framework. To disentangle the role of Kondo-type 4f - d hybridisation, calculations are performed with the RE 4f electrons treated both as frozen core states and explicitly in the valence manifold, enabling a direct assessment of their contribution to the band structure, dielectric function, and phonon dispersion. Spin-polarised calculations reveal significant spin-channel asymmetry, with magnetic moments reaching up to 30 μB per formula unit for select members of the series. The results establish a unified picture of how 4f occupancy and octahedral distortion collectively determine the magnetic and optoelectronic potential of this double-perovskite family.
summary: This theoretical study investigates the electronic, magnetic, and optical properties of $	ext{RE}_2	ext{MnNiO}_6$ double-perovskites using DFT+U. By systematically varying the rare earth element, the authors demonstrate how lattice contraction and $4f-d$ hybridization govern the material's potential for optoelectronic applications. The work provides a comprehensive picture of structure-property relationships in this class of correlated oxides.
main_problem: To systematically study the optoelectronic and magnetic properties of the $	ext{RE}_2	ext{MnNiO}_6$ double-perovskite oxide family, focusing on the influence of the A-site ionic radius via lanthanide contraction.
main_result: The study establishes a unified picture showing that the magnetic and optoelectronic potential of these materials is determined by the interplay between $4f$ electron occupancy and octahedral distortion.
method: The research employs first-principles calculations using the DFT+U framework, analyzing electronic structure, lattice dynamics, and optical properties.
model_or_system: The physical system is the $	ext{RE}_2	ext{MnNiO}_6$ double-perovskite oxide, where $	ext{RE}$ ranges from $	ext{La}$ to $	ext{Lu}$. The calculations treat the challenging $4f$ electrons both as frozen core states and explicitly in the valence manifold.
key_observables: Electronic band structure, dielectric function, phonon dispersion, magnetic moments (up to $30 \mu_B$), and optical absorption edges.
important_parameters: A-site ionic radius (governing lanthanide contraction), DFT+U parameters ($U=4 	ext{ eV}, J=5 	ext{ eV}$), and temperature (for thermodynamic analysis).
main_assumptions: The calculations assume the monoclinic $P2_1/c$ phase is the most relevant structure, and the methodology must account for the strong localization challenge of $4f$ electrons.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the crystal structure ($	ext{LMNO}$), phonon band structures showing $	ext{Re}$ dependence, and comprehensive optical properties ($\varepsilon(\omega)$, $\alpha(\omega)$, $R(\omega)$) across the series.
paper_structure: The paper follows a systematic computational approach: first establishing the structural and electronic ground state using DFT+U, then analyzing lattice dynamics and thermal properties, and finally detailing the optical response, all while tracking the systematic changes across the lanthanide series.
why_interesting: The explicit treatment of $4f$ electrons and the investigation of hybridization effects in transition metal oxides directly addresses strong electronic correlations, which is central to understanding many correlated materials.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 3. Band structures of Re2MnNiO6, where Re stands for (a)La, (b)Ce, (c)Pr, (d)Nd, (e)Pm, (f)Sm, (g)Eu, (h)Gd, (i)Tb, (j)Dy, (k)Ho, (l)Er, (m)Tm, (n)Yb, (o)Lu and (p)Y, without considering f-electrons in valence band.
FIG. 4. Band structures of Re2MnNiO6, where Re stands for (a)Ce, (b)Pr, (c)Nd, (d)Pm, (e)Sm, (f)Eu, (g)Gd, (h)Tb, (i)Dy, (j)Ho, (k)Er, (k)Tm, (m)Yb and (n)Lu with f-electrons in valence band.
FIG. 5. Phonon band structures of Re2MnNiO6, where Re stands for (a)La, (b)Ce, (c)Pr, (d)Nd, (e)Pm, (f)Sm, (g)Eu, (h)Gd, (i)Tb, (j)Dy, (k)Ho, (l)Er, (m)Tm, (n)Yb, (o)Lu and (p)Y.
